name: Ku
description: Ku, in Hawaiian mythology, is one of the four great gods (with Kane, Lono, and Kanaloa). He is the god of war, strength, male creativity, and politics. Ku is invoked for success in battle, agriculture, fishing, and building. He represents the procreative power of the male and is often associated with the rising sun and the east. Human sacrifice was sometimes practiced in Ku's name, particularly during times of war or societal upheaval. Ku's origins are deeply intertwined with the creation of the world in Hawaiian cosmology. He is believed to have played a vital role in separating the sky from the earth, bringing light and order to the primordial darkness. As a powerful and multifaceted deity, Ku embodies the raw energy and potential for both creation and destruction, representing a vital force in the Hawaiian pantheon and the lives of the people who worshipped him. He is often depicted as a fierce warrior, adorned with feathers and wielding weapons, symbolizing his dominion over war and his unwavering strength. He is also associated with the forest, where he provides sustenance and resources for the people. Ku's influence extends to all aspects of life, from the battlefield to the fields, making him a central figure in Hawaiian society and spirituality.
subtitle: God of War, Strength, and Male Creativity

keyMyths.title: Ku and Hina
keyMyths.description: Ku and Hina are often seen as complementary forces. Their relationship represents the balance between male and female energies in the universe.

keyMyths.title: Human Sacrifice to Ku
keyMyths.description: In times of war, human sacrifices were sometimes offered to Ku to ensure victory. This practice highlights the intense devotion and fear associated with this powerful deity.

keyMyths.title: Ku as a Creator God
keyMyths.description: Ku helped separate the sky and the earth, bringing light and order to the world. He is credited with the creation of many things.

sources.author: Martha Warren Beckwith
